name: Generic bounded wildcard for reading
question_text: You want to write a method that reads elements from a list of Number or any subtype (Integer, Double, etc.). Which parameter type best expresses this?
general_feedback: The PECS rule (Producer Extends, Consumer Super) says use ? extends T when reading from a collection (it produces Ts). List<? extends Number> lets the method accept List<Integer>, List<Double>, etc. but forbids adding non-null elements.
correct_feedback: Correct — Producer Extends — accept any List of Number or subtype for reading.

answers.feedback: Producer Extends — accept any List of Number or subtype for reading.
Invariance — List<Number> does NOT accept List<Integer>.
? super is for consuming (writing), not reading a Number subtype.
Too loose — you couldn't even guarantee elements are Numbers.
